# UC-20.2.9 · Cloud Commitment and Savings Plan Utilization

## Description

Underutilized commitments or savings plans leave money on the table. Monitoring utilization and coverage supports optimization and renewal decisions.

## Value

Underutilized commitments or savings plans leave money on the table. Monitoring utilization and coverage supports optimization and renewal decisions.

## Implementation

Ingest commitment and savings plan usage from cloud cost APIs. Alert when utilization or coverage drops below target. Report on commitment ROI and recommend size changes at renewal.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: AWS Cost Explorer, Azure Cost Management, CUDRI/savings plan data.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Commitment usage, savings plan coverage, hourly coverage %.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest commitment and savings plan usage from cloud cost APIs. Alert when utilization or coverage drops below target. Report on commitment ROI and recommend size changes at renewal.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud_cost sourcetype="aws:savings_plan"
| stats latest(utilization_pct) as util_pct, latest(coverage_pct) as coverage by plan_id, commitment_type
| where util_pct < 80 OR coverage < 70
| table plan_id, commitment_type, util_pct, coverage
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cloud Commitment and Savings Plan Utilization** — Underutilized commitments or savings plans leave money on the table. Monitoring utilization and coverage supports optimization and renewal decisions.

Documented **Data sources**: Commitment usage, savings plan coverage, hourly coverage %. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): AWS Cost Explorer, Azure Cost Management, CUDRI/savings plan data. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud_cost; **sourcetype**: aws:savings_plan. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud_cost, sourcetype="aws:savings_plan". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by plan_id, commitment_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where util_pct < 80 OR coverage < 70` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Cloud Commitment and Savings Plan Utilization**): table plan_id, commitment_type, util_pct, coverage


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with Azure Cost Management; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (utilization %), Table (plans below target), Line chart (coverage trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=cloud_cost sourcetype="aws:savings_plan"
| stats latest(utilization_pct) as util_pct, latest(coverage_pct) as coverage by plan_id, commitment_type
| where util_pct < 80 OR coverage < 70
| table plan_id, commitment_type, util_pct, coverage
```

## Visualization

Gauge (utilization %), Table (plans below target), Line chart (coverage trend).
